title: taraxacum subcrispum
intro: Taraxacum subcrispum, also known as the rough-leaved dandelion, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus, characterized by its rough, hairy leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Taraxacum subcrispum include its ability to support liver and kidney function, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
